name: Uplink Scheduling & Power Control
description: Sub-sector template for uplink scheduling, grant-based/configured-grant transmission, and uplink power control patents. Covers SR/BSR procedures, configured grants for URLLC, and fractional power control.
contextDescription: Uplink scheduling controls how UEs transmit data to the base station. In LTE/5G NR, the gNB grants uplink resources via DCI. For URLLC, configured grants (Type 1/Type 2) allow periodic transmission without dynamic scheduling. Power control (open-loop + closed-loop) is mandatory per 3GPP TS 38.213. Key targets: Qualcomm (modem), Apple (baseband), all smartphone OEMs. Buffer status reports (BSR) and scheduling requests (SR) are exercised continuously by every connected device.
scoringGuidance: Every connected UE performs uplink scheduling — BSR and SR are sent continuously.
Configured grant (semi-persistent scheduling) patents are critical for URLLC and industrial IoT.
Power control patents (TPC commands, path loss estimation) are mandatory in every UE — broad SEP surface.
Uplink multi-antenna (codebook-based precoding, SRS transmission) patents are high-value for 5G NR.
Grant-free access patents enable ultra-low latency industrial applications.

questions.question: What uplink scheduling mechanism does this patent address? Score 8-10 for configured grants (Type 1/2) or grant-free transmission, 5-7 for dynamic scheduling improvements (DCI-based grants), 3-4 for BSR/SR optimization, 1-2 for general uplink concepts.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Configured grants are the newest and most valuable UL scheduling mechanism for URLLC.

questions.question: Does this patent address uplink power control (open-loop, closed-loop, TPC commands)? Score 8-10 for novel power control algorithms (fractional, multi-component), 5-7 for standard power control improvements, 3-4 for power-aware scheduling, 1-2 for no power control aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: Power control is mandatory — every UE adjusts transmission power per gNB commands.

questions.question: Does this patent address multi-antenna uplink transmission (codebook-based precoding, SRS, UL MIMO)? Score 8-10 for novel UL MIMO or SRS enhancement, 5-7 for codebook selection optimization, 3-4 for single-antenna with diversity, 1-2 for no multi-antenna aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: UL MIMO is increasingly important in 5G NR for throughput and coverage.

questions.question: How closely does this patent align with 3GPP standard uplink procedures (TS 38.321/38.213/38.214)? Score 8-10 for direct implementation of standardized UL behavior, 5-7 for enabling standard compliance, 3-4 for standard-adjacent, 1-2 for proprietary.
questions.reasoningPrompt: UL scheduling is heavily standardized — identify specific 3GPP procedures.

questions.question: Does this patent enable ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) in the uplink? Score 8-10 for sub-ms UL latency techniques or reliability mechanisms, 5-7 for latency-aware scheduling, 3-4 for general QoS awareness, 1-2 for no URLLC aspect.
questions.reasoningPrompt: URLLC uplink is critical for industrial IoT, autonomous vehicles, and remote surgery.

questions.question: How detectable is this uplink scheduling innovation? Score 8-10 for behavior observable in UL signaling (PUSCH grants, UCI, SRS), 5-7 for behavior inferable from UL throughput or conformance testing, 3-4 for internal baseband processing, 1-2 for algorithm-level only.
questions.reasoningPrompt: UL grants and power control commands are observable in over-the-air captures.
